questions.q: Define arithmetic mean and give its formula.
questions.answer: Arithmetic mean is the sum of all observations divided by the number of observations. It is calculated using the formula: A = Σxi / N, where A is the arithmetic mean, xi is each observation, and N is the number of observations.

questions.q: What is the difference between population and sample mean?
questions.answer: Population mean is the average of all the observations in a population, whereas sample mean is the average of a subset of observations selected from the population. Population mean is denoted by μ, while sample mean is denoted by x̄.

questions.q: Explain the concept of weighted mean.
questions.answer: Weighted mean is a type of average that gives more importance to certain observations based on their weights. It is calculated using the formula: W = (Σxi * wi) / (Σwi), where W is the weighted mean, xi is each observation, and wi is the corresponding weight.

questions.q: Define median and explain its importance.
questions.answer: Median is the middle value of a dataset when it is arranged in ascending or descending order. It is an important measure of central tendency as it is not affected by extreme values and is used in skewed distributions.

questions.q: What is the difference between median and mode?
questions.answer: Median is the middle value of a dataset, while mode is the most frequently occurring value. The difference between median and mode is that median is affected by the position of the middle value, while mode is affected by the frequency of the value.

questions.q: Explain the concept of mode and its types.
questions.answer: Mode is the most frequently occurring value in a dataset. There can be multiple modes in a dataset, and they can be classified into unimodal, bimodal, and multimodal distributions.

questions.q: Define and explain the concept of mid-point.
questions.answer: Mid-point is the average of the highest and lowest values in a dataset. It is used as an estimate of the median and is calculated using the formula: Mid-point = (Highest value + Lowest value) / 2.

questions.q: Explain the concept of range and its importance.
questions.answer: Range is the difference between the highest and lowest values in a dataset. It is an important measure of dispersion as it gives an idea of the spread of the data and is used in data analysis.

questions.q: What is the difference between absolute mean deviation and relative mean deviation?
questions.answer: Absolute mean deviation is the average of the absolute difference between each observation and the mean, while relative mean deviation is the average of the relative difference between each observation and the mean. Relative mean deviation is a standardized measure of dispersion.

questions.q: Explain the concept of quartile and its importance.
questions.answer: Quartile is a measure of central tendency that divides a dataset into four equal parts. It is an important measure as it gives an idea of the spread of the data and is used in data analysis.
